title: pinaropappus diguetii
intro: Pinaropappus diguetii, also known as the Resin Bush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds found in its resin. The resin has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from coughs and colds. Pinaropappus diguetii's resin can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, salves, and infusions, which can be prepared by steeping the plant material in hot water or mixing it with other herbs to create a topical application.
